Title: Test adding a new signal
Preconditions: The system is initialized and the GestoreSegnalazioni object is available.
Test Data: A new signal with valid attributes (id, title, description, date, utente).
Test Steps:
1. Call the addSegnalazione(Segnalazione) operation on the GestoreSegnalazioni object.
2. Pass the new signal as an argument.
3. Verify that the signal is added to the list of signals.
Expected Result: The new signal is added to the list of signals.

Title: Test modifying an existing signal
Preconditions: The system is initialized, and the GestoreSegnalazioni object is available. A signal with valid attributes already exists.
Test Data: A modified signal with valid attributes.
Test Steps:
1. Call the modifySegnalazione(Segnalazione, int) operation on the GestoreSegnalazioni object.
2. Pass the modified signal and the index of the signal to be modified as arguments.
3. Verify that the signal is modified correctly.

Title: Test removing a signal
Preconditions: The system is initialized, and the GestoreSegnalazioni object is available. A signal with valid attributes exists.
Test Data: The index of the signal to be removed.
Test Steps:
1. Call the removeSegnalazione(int) operation on the GestoreSegnalazioni object.
2. Pass the index of the signal to be removed as an argument.
3. Verify that the signal is removed from the list of signals.
Expected Result: The signal is removed from the list of signals.

Title: Test getting user attributes
Preconditions: The system is initialized, and the Utente object is available.
Test Steps:
1. Call the getSPart() operation on the Utente object.
2. Verify that the departure station is returned.
3. Call the getSArr() operation on the Utente object.
4. Verify that the arrival station is returned.
5. Call the getSede() operation on the Utente object.
6. Verify that the user location is returned.
7. Call the calcMToStation() operation on the Utente object.
8. Verify that the minimum travel time is calculated correctly.
Expected Result: The user attributes are returned correctly, and the minimum travel time is calculated correctly.

Title: Test setting calendar data
Preconditions: The system is initialized, and the Calendario object is available.
Test Steps:
1. Call the setCalendario(Calendario) operation on the Calendario object.
2. Pass the new calendar data as an argument.
3. Verify that the calendar data is set correctly.
Expected Result: The calendar data is set correctly.

Title: Test getting calendar data
Preconditions: The system is initialized, and the Calendario object is available.
Test Steps:
1. Call the getCalendario() operation on the Calendario object.
2. Verify that the calendar data is returned correctly.
Expected Result: The calendar data is returned correctly.

Title: Test getting calendar data
Preconditions: The system is initialized, and the GestoreCalendario object is available.
Test Steps:
1. Call the getCalendario() operation on the GestoreCalendario object.
2. Verify that the calendar data is returned correctly.
Expected Result: The calendar data is returned correctly.

Title: Test updating calendar data
Preconditions: The system is initialized, and the GestoreCalendario object is available.
Test Steps:
1. Call the updateCalendario(Calendario) operation on the GestoreCalendario object.
2. Pass the updated calendar data as an argument.
3. Verify that the calendar data is updated correctly.
Expected Result: The calendar data is updated correctly.

Title: Test getting train information
Preconditions: The system is initialized, and the GestoreTreni object is available.
Test Steps:
1. Call the getTrainInfo(int id) operation on the GestoreTreni object.
2. Pass the train ID as an argument.
3. Verify that the train information is returned correctly.
Expected Result: The train information is returned correctly.

Title: Test deleting preferred train
Preconditions: The system is initialized, and the GestoreTreni object is available. A preferred train exists.
Test Data: The index of the preferred train to be deleted.
Test Steps:
1. Call the deletePrefTrain(int) operation on the GestoreTreni object.
2. Pass the index of the preferred train to be deleted as an argument.
3. Verify that the preferred train is deleted correctly.
Expected Result: The preferred train is deleted correctly.

Title: Test getting train variations
Preconditions: The system is initialized, and the GestoreVariazioni object is available.
Test Steps:
1. Call the GetVariazioni() operation on the GestoreVariazioni object.
2. Verify that the list of train variations is returned correctly.
Expected Result: The list of train variations is returned correctly.

Title: Test getting train variations for a specific train
Preconditions: The system is initialized, and the GestoreVariazioni object is available.
Test Steps:
1. Call the GetVariazioni(Treno) operation on the GestoreVariazioni object.
2. Pass the train object as an argument.
3. Verify that the list of train variations for the specified train is returned correctly.
Expected Result: The list of train variations for the specified train is returned correctly.

Title: Test getting train variations for a specific station
Preconditions: The system is initialized, and the GestoreVariazioni object is available.
Test Steps:
1. Call the GetVariazioni(Stazione) operation on the GestoreVariazioni object.
2. Pass the station object as an argument.
3. Verify that the list of train variations for the specified station is returned correctly.
Expected Result: The list of train variations for the specified station is returned correctly.

Title: Test getting notifications
Preconditions: The system is initialized, and the SistemaNotifica object is available. Train variations exist.
Test Steps:
1. Call the Start() operation on the SistemaNotifica object.
2. Verify that the notifications are displayed correctly.

Title: Test API communication
Preconditions: The system is initialized, and the API object is available.
Test Data: Valid user credentials (email, password) for login and a valid calendar for train scheduling.
Test Steps:
1. Call the FirebaseAuth.getInstance() operation on the API object.
2. Verify that the Firebase Authentication instance is returned correctly.
3. Call the mAuth.signInWithEmailAndPassword(email, password) operation on the API object.
4. Pass the user email and password as arguments.
5. Verify that the user is logged in successfully.
6. Call the getCalendario() operation on the Sistema gestione dati object.
7. Verify that the calendar data is returned correctly.
Expected Result: The user is logged in successfully, and the calendar data is returned correctly.
